title: Sith Jedi Statue
content:
{{Top|leg}}
[[File:Sith_Jedi.jpg|thumb|right|250px|A pair of the statues.]]
The '''Sith Jedi Statues''' were statues on [[Dromund Kaas/Legends|Dromund Kaas]], inside the [[Dark Force Temple]]. The Temple was once inhabited by the [[Prophets of the Dark Side]], known practitioners of [[Sith alchemy/Legends|Sith alchemy]].

The Jedi statues of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] were always motionless and steady, as with [[Unidentified Sith statues|the temple's Sith statues]], and held a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. Created as guardians against intruders, their stone bodies were animated by [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] when trespassers got too near, presumably through some sort of [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith magic]]. On alien presence, the lightsaber was ignited and they came to life like stone automatons to fight the intruder.

Any non-lightsaber wielder who met them was doomed since the Dark Force aura of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] meddled with all mechanical weapons except lightsabers.

==Behind the scenes==
While the name ''Sith Jedi'' seems awkward, being an [[Wikipedia:Oxymoron|oxymoron]], this is the production name of the enemy unit in the ''Mysteries of the Sith'' official site. There is not enough information on their origins or nature to challenge its naming.

The in-game statue models themselves were based visually on the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Yun]] and [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]] from ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]'', using the same animations and similar textures.

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Mysteries of the Sith]]''
